items.text:
Audition Notice “A Midsummer Night’s Dream”
## **Audition Notice**

**“A Midsummer Night’s Dream”**
 By: William Shakespeare
 Directed By: Kytriena Payseno & Gwydion Winkelmeyer

Stage Manager: Wilma Spencer

Produced By: Lyons Community Theater 

Hosted By: The Town of Lyons

**Mission Statement**

LCT exists to establish and promote live theater and other performing arts in order to contribute to the quality of life in Lyons and to build community in doing so.

**Values Statement**

Lyons Community Theatre (LCT) brings people together through the shared joy of storytelling, creativity, and community connection. As a locally rooted, volunteer-powered organization, we create space for residents of all ages and backgrounds to participate in every aspect of theatre—from performing and directing to building sets and supporting behind the scenes.

Our mission is to strengthen community bonds, celebrate local talent, and make the performing arts accessible, inclusive, and welcoming for all. Through engaging productions, hands-on learning opportunities, and meaningful collaborations, LCT provides a place where friendships are formed, creativity flourishes, and everyone has a role to play.

We believe that theatre should reflect the heart of our town—bold, compassionate, connected, and deeply human.

**Auditions:**

Please email a recording of your chosen reading or monologue to [kytriena.lyonscommtheater@gmail.com](mailto:kytriena.lyonscommtheater@gmail.com) 

Due Saturday, May 16th, 2026 @Midnight

Link to [script](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1x9Md7MPKL3ORGgVVCboLAYZzowidZXvF/view?usp=sharing): [https://drive.google.com/file/d/1x9Md7MPKL3ORGgVVCboLAYZzowidZXvF/view?usp=sharing](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1x9Md7MPKL3ORGgVVCboLAYZzowidZXvF/view?usp=sharing) 

**Performance Dates:**
* **Tech Week:** August 9–13, 2026 (Sunday–Thursday)
* **Performances:** August 14 & 15, 2026 (6:30pm) 
* **Strike:** August 15, 2026 (All required to help)

Location: Sandstone Park, 350 4th Ave, Lyons, CO 80540

**Audition Information:**

Roles are **Non-Union | Unpaid**

*LCT encourages actors of all races, ethnicities, genders, ages, and abilities to audition. We welcome performers from all backgrounds.*

Auditioners must sign up in advance.

**All auditioners must fill out the audition form. If it is not filled out prior, the auditioner will need to fill it out twice.**

**[Lyons Community Theatre Audition Form](https://forms.gle/AskLHkKGqSP2ET3K8)****: **[https://forms.gle/AskLHkKGqSP2ET3K8](https://forms.gle/AskLHkKGqSP2ET3K8)** **

**Summary of *****A Midsummer Night’s Dream***
*   A Midsummer Night’s Dream* by William Shakespeare is a whimsical comedy about love, magic, and mistaken identity. The story weaves together four young lovers from Athens, a group of amateur actors rehearsing a play, and a mischievous band of fairies living in an enchanted forest.
   When the lovers flee into the woods, they become entangled in a magical feud between the fairy king and queen. With the help (and interference) of the playful sprite Puck, spells are cast that cause people to fall in and out of love in hilarious and unexpected ways. Meanwhile, the bumbling actors prepare a performance that adds even more humor to the tale.
   In the end, the chaos resolves into harmony, and the play celebrates the unpredictable, irrational, and joyful nature of love.
**Character Breakdown / Casting Notes**

* Oberon – King of the Fairies

items.text:
Hi, I'm Dani, a film photographer and part of Flare, an analog photography collective for women and dissidences with presence in Chile, France and Canada. 

We run open calls, photography gatherings and collaborative editorials where different artistic practices intersect — and we're hoping to bring it all together in a photobook in the future. We're now looking to connect with people in Berlin and build something here too — so if you're into analog photography, this is your invitation 💛

This Sunday May 10th we're opening a Mother's Day call: desired motherhood, not-so-desired, imposed, dissident. All perspectives welcome — emotional, conceptual, abstract, entirely your own. Analog format only.

Form closes Sunday May 10th:
🕛 12:00 Santiago & Valdivia, Chile
🕛 12:00 Toronto, Canada 
🕕 19:00 Toulouse, France 
🕕 19:00 Berlin, Germany 

📋 English form: https://forms.gle/j2DnPfsZRfwqm8je6
📋 Español: https://forms.gle/CELnomRbLHqZ2EB26
📋 Français: https://forms.gle/4dnp2pZSfec7LDTh7 

✶

Photography: https://www.instagram.com/jopipemi

✶

https://www.instagram.com/flare.analogo/

items.text:
Hi everyone!

I'm a photographer based in Berlin and I'm planning a series of fashion shoots around the city in the next few months, and am looking for fresh, up and coming Make Up Artists, Hair Stylists and Stylists interested in joining these projects.

These are tfp shoots, which is why they're a good occasion for younger talents to join me and my fun lovely little team! :)

Just hit me up if you're interested!

Cheers

(you can find my work on lalefavre.com)

items.summary:
Hi everyone!

I'm a photographer based in Berlin and I'm planning a series of fashion shoots around the city in the next few months, and am looking for fresh, up and coming Make Up Artists, Hair Stylists and Stylists interested in joining these projects.

These are tfp shoots, which is why they're a good occasion for younger talents to join me and my fun lovely little team! :)

Just hit me up if you're interested!

Cheers

(you can find my work on lalefavre.com)

items.text:
Every session at V Artist Talent Center is built to challenge creativity, strengthen performance, and develop confidence on stage and on camera. From emotional delivery to character immersion, our intensive acting workshops are designed to help aspiring artists sharpen their craft through real training experiences.

Join us every Saturday at V Artist Talent Center, located at Archerub Building, 3rd Floor, and experience hands-on acting workshops guided to help you grow as a performer. Be part of a community that is passionate about learning, expression, and artistic development.

Contact us today and be part of our growing Family.
📞+639681998080
📩 vfilm@gmail.com
🌐 https://vmediaprod.com
📍 Timog Ave. cor. EDSA, Quezon City (Front of GMA Network)
#VAdvertisingInc.
#advertisingagency

items.groupTitle: Casting Calls & Acting Auditions in London / UK
items.summary:
Every session at V Artist Talent Center is built to challenge creativity, strengthen performance, and develop confidence on stage and on camera. From emotional delivery to character immersion, our intensive acting workshops are designed to help aspiring artists sharpen their craft through real training experiences.

Join us every Saturday at V Artist Talent Center, located at Archerub Building, 3rd Floor, and experience hands-on acting workshops guided to help you grow as a performer. Be part of a community that is passionate about learning, expression, and artistic development.

Contact us today and be part of our growing Family.
📞+639681998080
📩 vfilm@gmail.com
🌐 https://vmediaprod.com
📍 Timog Ave. cor. EDSA, Quezon City (Front of GMA Network)
#VAdvertisingInc.
#advertisingagency

items.text:
CASTING

ROLE - ATTACKER DAX

The attacker version of the lead male, he is masked the entire show and his face is never revealed. Lots of stage time and a vital role in the play.

Experience with stage combat and/or physical theatre preferred.

FULL LENGTH SUSPENSEFUL ROMANTIC HORROR PLAY.

Looking for someone Male, slim/muscly, 6ft 2” or 6ft 3” with brown hair to match the lead male.

Ethnicity: White 

Required Media: Headshot/Photo, Video Reel, Cover Letter

LONDON, UK ONLY.

ALL INFORMATION ON THE POSTER

Payment - Profit share, equal split between all 3 actors and the backstage crew/tech.

Dates - Rehearsals every week on a Sunday from June (dates to be confirmed), 2 week run in a London theatre (or 2) over Halloween (exactly dates to follow).

To apply send a DM or email info@distortedrebels.com

items.text:
CASTING CALL — PAID EDITORIAL PORTRAIT SHOOT

Los Angeles / Hollywood | May 16–19 (exact date TBD) | 4–6 Hours | $150

Seeking: Male talent, 40s–60s

This is a cinematic portrait shoot, not a commercial job. The images are being made as authored editorial work — the kind of photograph that lives on a wall or in a book, not an ad. You will receive full high-resolution images for your own portfolio, acting reel, or personal use.

We are looking for someone who can inhabit a character, not just stand in front of a camera. Presence matters more than looks. The right person has a face that carries history — heavy, weathered, specific. Think less model, more character actor. Early Sopranos energy. The kind of man who looks like he’s been somewhere and isn’t sure where he’s going next.

The shoot takes place at a Hollywood motel. The work is psychological and still — influenced by photographer Philip-Lorca diCorcia. Not fashion. Not commercial. Something quieter and more interior.

The primary setup: a man on the edge of a bed, business suit on, briefcase in hand or at his feet, staring into the corner of the room. That’s the image. Simple, loaded, cinematic. We’ll work through 2–3 variations within the same emotional world.

This role requires stillness and the ability to hold an internal emotional state without performing it. If you’ve ever been told your face does the work — this is that job.

Ideal if you own a dark business suit. Well-worn is better than sharp. Wardrobe can be supplied if needed — just include your measurements when you submit.

Rate: $150 for the session

Full image credit on all published work

High-res selects provided for your portfolio

To submit: send a recent photo (doesn’t need to be professional, just natural) and a brief note about yourself to info@leonardabraham.com

items.text:
A fun fact: casting agents often spend less than ten seconds watching a showreel, and may form their first impression in five.

That’s why a long four-minute monologue is usually pointless. Agents skim through footage searching for extremes — laughter, tears, fear, rage — because strong emotions reveal whether a face truly lives on camera.

So instead of worrying about an elaborate reel with long dialogue scenes, create a super-short, well-lit mini-reel focused on expressions and emotional range. Have a fun Joan Crawford moment. Make Charles Laughton run for his money. If those few seconds spark curiosity, the casting agent will come back for more.

This is my mini-reel from my showreel master class at the Baltic Film and Media Institute.

items.summary:
A fun fact: casting agents often spend less than ten seconds watching a showreel, and may form their first impression in five.

That’s why a long four-minute monologue is usually pointless. Agents skim through footage searching for extremes — laughter, tears, fear, rage — because strong emotions reveal whether a face truly lives on camera.

So instead of worrying about an elaborate reel with long dialogue scenes, create a super-short, well-lit mini-reel focused on expressions and emotional range. Have a fun Joan Crawford moment. Make Charles Laughton run for his money. If those few seconds spark curiosity, the casting agent will come back for more.

This is my mini-reel from my showreel master class at the Baltic Film and Media Institute.
items.title: A fun fact: casting agents often spend less than ten seconds watching a showreel, and may form their first impression in five.

items.text:
Lige færdig med at forberede min antiinflammatoriske flydende guld til ugen. Siden jeg begyndte at drikke den, er mine ledsmerter nede, og min energi er i topform.
Ikke mere morgenstivhed, og min mave føles fantastisk!
Hvem vil have hele opskriften? Skriv et "JA" i kommentarerne, så deler jeg præcis, hvad der er indeni.
